claim: Lithium damages function in the SCA1 mouse model

result.claim: Lithium damages function in the SCA1 mouse model
result.reasoning: The claim that "Lithium damages function in the SCA1 mouse model" is strongly refuted by the available evidence. Paper W2048966304, titled "Lithium Therapy Improves Neurological Function and Hippocampal Dendritic Arborization in a Spinocerebellar Ataxia Type 1 Mouse Model" (2007), directly contradicts this claim. Its abstract states that lithium has a "positive effect on multiple behavioral measures and hippocampal neuropathology in an authentic disease model" of SCA1. This indicates an improvement, not damage, to function. No propositions or chunks were found that supported the original claim, while the most relevant paper explicitly states the opposite.

evidence.text: VPA and lithium both induce significant down-regulation of group I CD1 expression during differentiation of human monocyte-derived immature DC.
evidence.source: Valproic Acid and Lithium Meditate Anti-Inflammatory Effects by Differentially Modulating Dendritic Cell Differentiation and Function.

evidence.text: Induced MI in mice leads to contractile dysfunction.
evidence.source: Homocysteine, Vitamins B6 and Folic Acid in Experimental Models of Myocardial Infarction and Heart Failure-How Strong Is That Link?

evidence.text: VPA and lithium both reduced the serum level of IL-10 after LPS challenge in mice.
evidence.source: Valproic Acid and Lithium Meditate Anti-Inflammatory Effects by Differentially Modulating Dendritic Cell Differentiation and Function.

evidence.text: Treatment with VPA or lithium effectively alleviated the severity of collagen-induced arthritis triggered by LPS in mice.
evidence.source: Valproic Acid and Lithium Meditate Anti-Inflammatory Effects by Differentially Modulating Dendritic Cell Differentiation and Function.

evidence.text: ClockΔ19 mice were employed to model bipolar disorder.
evidence.source: Hippocampal transcriptome analysis in ClockΔ19 mice identifies pathways associated with glial cell differentiation and myelination.

evidence.text: Barrier function was further disrupted in the ileum of GCC null mice after a low dose LPS challenge.
evidence.source: Loss of guanylyl cyclase C (GCC) signaling leads to dysfunctional intestinal barrier.

evidence.text: VPA and lithium both reduced the serum level of IL-6 after LPS challenge in mice.
evidence.source: Valproic Acid and Lithium Meditate Anti-Inflammatory Effects by Differentially Modulating Dendritic Cell Differentiation and Function.

evidence.text: These effects were also observed in the severe SMA model SMNΔ7 mouse.
evidence.source: Glial activation and central synapse loss, but not motoneuron degeneration, are prevented by the sigma-1 receptor agonist PRE-084 in the Smn2B/- mouse model of spinal muscular atrophy

evidence.text: DNA damage has been detected in the G93A mouse model of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).
evidence.source: Wild-type p53-induced phosphatase 1 down-regulation promotes apoptosis by activating the DNA damage-response pathway in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

evidence.text: The experimental tMCAO mouse model showed exacerbated degeneration of long-term sensorimotor performance.
evidence.source: FGF21, a modulator of astrocyte reactivity, protects against ischemic brain injury through anti-inflammatory and neurotrophic pathways.
